mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand. Higher GDP feeds into corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 level on 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises financing costs and discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% over the test period, or mean return is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payroll employment and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and labor income, driving corporate earnings and positive equity returns. The joint signal amplifies confidence in the expansion.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS are above their 12-month moving averages, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, unemployment at cycle lows combined with rising initial claims signals impending recession. Forward returns over the next quarter turn negative as labor market slack re-emerges.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion: UNRATE below 4.0% and rising by more than 0.3pp over 6 months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of detected late-cycle regimes, or the signal yields negative hit rate over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects manufacturing expansion and robust economic output. Positive monthly INDPRO surprises translate into short-term equity bullish momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day rolling correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is consistently negative or below 0.1 over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts exceed 1200 thousand units, the residential construction sector is expanding strongly, boosting GDP and related industries. This drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The mean forward 60-day return following HOUST > 1200 is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates that monetary tightening is effectively cooling the rate-sensitive housing sector. This joint deterioration signals a broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase AND HOUST month-over-month decrease
failure_condition: The joint occurrence (FEDFUNDS rising + HOUST falling) yields positive forward 20-day returns more than 40% of the time over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated (above 260 index level) but stable, equity markets have acclimated to the price level and returns are driven by real economic momentum rather than inflation surprises. The market looks through high inflation provided it is not accelerating.
regime_hint: high inflation plateau: CPIAUCSL > 260 and year-over-year change < 4%
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 260 and YoY inflation is below 4%, forward 20-day returns are negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
